# **Introduction** {#introduction}

### **Welcome to Project 2030: MyAI Future Hackathon\!** {#welcome-to-project-2030:-myai-future-hackathon!}

**Project 2030: MyAI Future Hackathon** is a strategic, national-scale initiative developed as a high-impact response to Malaysia’s evolving digital landscape. Spearheaded by **Google Developer Groups (GDG) On Campus UTM**, this movement serves as a high-intensity intervention to bridge the critical talent gap between academic theory and the rapid execution requirements of the global Artificial Intelligence economy.

## 

## **Strategic Alignment & National Agenda** {#strategic-alignment-&-national-agenda}

Project 2030 is engineered as a dedicated response to national requirements, directly materializing the blueprints of the **Malaysia Madani** framework, **MyDIGITAL** and the **New Industrial Master Plan (NIMP) 2030**. By aligning local innovation with global enterprise standards, the initiative ensures that the next generation of technical talent is prepared to lead Malaysia toward its goal of becoming a high-income, tech-driven nation.

### 

### **Technological Sovereignty: From Consumption to Creation** {#technological-sovereignty:-from-consumption-to-creation}

The core mission of Project 2030 is the pursuit of **Technological Sovereignty**. Historically, the national ecosystem has been characterized by the consumption of global technology platforms. This initiative initiates a paradigm shift, transitioning participants from passive technology consumers into **Sovereign Technology Builders**.

By mastering the construction of indigenous digital infrastructure, the talent pool ensures that the logic governing future technologies is homegrown and remains strictly aligned with national interests and local needs.

### 

### 

### 

### 

### 

### 

### **The 'Build with AI' Mandate** {#the-'build-with-ai'-mandate}

Anchored within the global **'Build with AI'** campaign, the program provides access to the **Google AI Ecosystem Stack**. Participants leverage the power of **Gemini 2.0** and **Vertex AI** to move beyond traditional generative models toward the mastery of **Agentic AI**—autonomous systems capable of reasoning, planning, and executing multi-step tasks.

The program is strictly grounded in **Google’s AI Principles**, emphasizing innovation that is both bold and responsible. The objective is to create local impact and utilize AI for the collective good of humanity, specifically addressing five "Grand Challenges":

* **Agrotechnology:** Strengthening food security through intelligent diagnostics.  
* **GovTech:** Eliminating bureaucratic friction and enhancing digital public services.  
* **Healthcare:** Redefining clinical workflows and wellbeing for an aging population.  
* **Smart Cities:** Optimizing mobility and infrastructure within regional innovation corridors.  
* **FinTech:** Engineering secure and resilient financial ecosystems.

### **The Developmental Pipeline** {#the-developmental-pipeline}

Project 2030 operates as a rigorous, stage-by-stage developmental journey. This phased approach ensures that by the final stage of the hackathon, participants are not merely coding prototypes, but engineering high-fidelity solutions ready for industry validation and real-world deployment.

# **MyAI Future Hackathon Timeline** {#myai-future-hackathon-timeline}

| Demo Day | 16th May 2026 |

# **The Grand Challenges:** {#the-grand-challenges:}

# **National Problem Tracks**

***Advancing the Nation***  
***by Building Solutions with Google AI***

The MyAI Future Hackathon is centered on five "Grand Challenges" derived from Malaysia’s National Key Result Areas (NKRAs). Participants must select one track to architect a solution that leverages the **Google AI Ecosystem Stack**.

### **Track 1: Padi & Plates (Agrotech & Food Security)** {#track-1:-padi-&-plates-(agrotech-&-food-security)}

* **Background:** Malaysia currently faces a multi-billion ringgit food import dependency, leaving the nation vulnerable to global supply chain fluctuations. Smallholder farmers, who form the backbone of this sector, suffer significant yield losses due to undetected crop pathologies and inefficient resource planning.

### **Track 2: Citizens First (GovTech & Digital Services)** {#track-2:-citizens-first-(govtech-&-digital-services)}

* **Background:** The transition to "End-to-End" digital government services is a core pillar of the **MyDIGITAL** blueprint. Despite progress, bureaucratic friction and manual document verification remain significant bottlenecks, while climate change has made annual floods a recurring national crisis.

### **Track 3: Vital Signs (Healthcare & Wellbeing)** {#track-3:-vital-signs-(healthcare-&-wellbeing)}

* **Background:** As Malaysia transitions toward "Aged Society" status, the public healthcare system faces unprecedented strain. Overcrowding in Emergency Departments and a shortage of home-care monitoring for the elderly are critical issues requiring immediate technological intervention.

### **Track 4: Green Horizon (Smart Cities & Mobility)** {#track-4:-green-horizon-(smart-cities-&-mobility)}

* **Background:** Urban centers, particularly the **Johor-Singapore Innovation Corridor**, face severe mobility congestion. Achieving Net Zero emissions by 2050 requires a fundamental shift in how transport systems are managed and how carbon footprints are tracked.

### **Track 5: Secure Digital (FinTech & Security)** {#track-5:-secure-digital-(fintech-&-security)}

* **Background:** Digital fraud and sophisticated scams result in millions of ringgit in losses for Malaysian citizens annually. Building a resilient digital economy requires advanced, real-time protection layers that can outpace increasingly automated threats.

### 

### 

### 

### **Technical Mandate: The "Build With AI" Requirement** {#technical-mandate:-the-"build-with-ai"-requirement}

To qualify for evaluation, every project must integrate the **Google AI Ecosystem Stack** as its core foundation. Solutions will be evaluated based on their transition from "Chat" (simple conversation) to **"Action" (Autonomous Execution)**.

1. **The Intelligence (Brain):** **Gemini 2.0** (Flash for speed/low-latency or Pro for complex reasoning).

2. **The Orchestrator:** **Vertex AI Agent Builder** and **Firebase Genkit** for building Agentic AI workflows.

3. **The Development Lifecycle:** **Google Cloud Workstations** for coding and **Google Cloud Run** for serverless deployment.

4. **The Context:** **Vertex AI Search** for grounded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using national or industrial datasets.

# **Rules & Regulations** {#rules-&-regulations}

By registering and participating, all team members unconditionally agree to abide by these Rules and Regulations in full. The organising committee reserves the right to amend these rules at any time; updates will be communicated via the official website and Discord channels. **Registration to participate in MyAI Future Hackathon is Free Of Charge.**

→ Registration Platform:  
	   Google Forms: [https://forms.gle/z3J5Z4n7f16jGYfr9](https://forms.gle/z3J5Z4n7f16jGYfr9)

**Section 1 — Eligibility** 

**Open to all Malaysian university students.** Participants must be currently enrolled (full-time or part-time) in a recognised Malaysian public or private university, polytechnic, or community college as of March 2026\. 

**All academic levels are welcome.** Undergraduate, diploma, and postgraduate students may participate. Recent graduates who completed their studies after **July 2025 are also eligible.** 

**Mixed-institution teams are allowed.** Team members may come from different universities or faculties. There is no restriction on the department or field of study. 

**Student ID required.** All participants must present a valid student ID or enrolment verification letter during registration and at the final event if selected as a Top 50 finalist. 

**Each participant may only join one team.** Registering with or contributing to multiple teams will result in disqualification of all teams involved. 

**Section 2 — Team Requirements** 

1. **Team size: Solo to 4 members.** Solo submissions are accepted. Teams with more than 4 members at the time of submission will be disqualified.   
2. **Designate one Team Lead.** The Team Lead is the primary point of contact for all official communications, submission confirmations, and prize disbursements.   
3. **Team composition is fixed after registration closes.** Members may not be added, removed, or substituted after the registration **deadline on 3 April 2026**. Any changes must be requested in writing before that date.   
4. **Mentors and advisors are not team members.** Faculty supervisors, industry mentors, or external coaches are welcome to guide teams but may not contribute directly to code, design, or submission materials.

**Section 3 — Submission Guidelines** 

1. **Submission deadline is 21 April 2026 at 11:59 PM.** This is a hard stop. No extensions will be granted under any circumstances. Late submissions will be automatically rejected by the system. 

   → Platform: Google Forms

   → Submission Link: To Be Announced

   → Submission Period: 3rd April 2026 \- 21 April 2026

2. **All deliverables must be submitted via the official submission portal.** Email submissions or links shared outside the portal will not be accepted. 

3. **Required submission package:** 

   → **GitHub Repository (public).** Must contain all source code, a detailed README, and setup instructions. 

   → **Video Demo (max 3 minutes).** Uploaded to YouTube or Google Drive (public/unlisted). Must demonstrate the working prototype. 

   → **Google Slide Link (max 15 slides, PDF).** Covering problem, solution, tech stack, impact, and business model.

   → **Prototype / Live App Link.** Must be accessible without login. Include test credentials if login is unavoidable. 

4. **All submissions must be in English.** Supporting materials in Bahasa Malaysia are permitted but the primary language of the deck and README must be English. 

5. **Projects must be built during the hackathon period. The hacking window opens on 15 March 2026\.** Pre-existing projects or projects started before this date are not permitted. 

**Section 4 — Code of Conduct & Plagiarism Policy** 

1. **Zero tolerance for plagiarism.** Copying code, designs, or content from other teams — or from previous hackathon projects — without attribution is grounds for immediate disqualification. All code will be screened for plagiarism.   
2. **AI-generated code must be disclosed.** Teams are permitted to use AI coding tools (GitHub Copilot, Gemini, etc.) but must declare this in the README. The team must be able to explain and defend every part of their codebase during judging.   
3. **Respectful conduct is mandatory.** Harassment, discrimination, or intimidation of any kind — based on gender, race, religion, nationality, disability, or any other characteristic — will not be tolerated and will result in immediate removal from the event.   
4. **No harassment policy.** Any participant who experiences or witnesses harassment must report it immediately to the organising committee. All reports will be treated confidentially.   
5. **Participants must attend mandatory sessions.** Finalists (Top 50\) are required to attend both days of the Final Event (16–17 May 2026\) in person. Failure to attend without prior written notice may result in disqualification.   
6. **Responsible use of AI.** In line with Talk 3 (Ethical/Responsible Use of AI, 11 April 2026), all projects must comply with ethical AI principles. Projects that exhibit bias, privacy violations, or potential for harm will be penalised during judging.

**Section 5 — Intellectual Property & Ownership** 

1. **Teams retain full ownership of their intellectual property.** Project 2030 does not claim ownership over any project, code, design, or idea submitted to the hackathon.   
2. **Licence to showcase.** By submitting, teams grant Project 2030 and its sponsors a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to showcase, promote, and reference their project in marketing materials, social media, and press coverage.   
3. **Open-source is encouraged but not required.** Teams are free to choose any licence for their code. If a project uses third-party open-source libraries, the team must comply with those libraries' licences.   
4. **Third-party assets must be properly licensed.** Teams may not use copyrighted images, music, fonts, or code without a valid licence or attribution. Violations may result in score deductions or disqualification. 

**Section 6 — Disqualification Conditions** 

1. **Late submission.** Any submission received after **21 April 2026 at 11:59 PM**.   
2. **Team size violation. More than 4 members** at the time of submission.   
3. **Plagiarism or academic dishonesty.** Copying code or content from another team or existing project without disclosure and attribution.   
4. **Misrepresentation.** Submitting a project that was not built during the hackathon period **(15 March – 21 April 2026).**   
5. **Code of conduct violation.** Harassment, discrimination, cheating, or any behaviour deemed harmful by the organising committee.   
6. **Ineligible participant.** A team member who does not meet the student eligibility criteria and whose participation was not disclosed during registration.   
7. **Failure to attend finals. Top 50 finalists** who do not attend the **Final Event (16–17 May 2026\)** without prior written approval from the committee. 

**Section 7 — Prizes & Judging Disclaimer** 

1. **Prize values are approximate and subject to change.** The organising committee reserves the right to adjust prize amounts, categories, or sponsors up to 7 days before the Final Event.   
2. **Judges' decisions are final.** The organising committee and judging panel's decisions are final and binding. No appeals or re-evaluations will be entertained after the Awards Ceremony.   
3. **Prizes are awarded to the team, not individuals.** Cash prizes and awards will be disbursed to the Team Lead. Distribution among team members is the sole responsibility of the team.   
4. **Track winners.** Special track prizes (e.g., GovTech, Health, Education) are awarded at the judges' discretion based on relevance and quality within that track.   
5. **Tax obligations.** Prize recipients are solely responsible for any applicable taxes or levies on prizes received. 

# **Judging Criteria**  {#judging-criteria}

### **Preliminary Judging Criteria Breakdown** {#preliminary-judging-criteria-breakdown}

| AI Implementation & Technical Execution (25 Marks) |  |
| :---: | ----- |
| **MAX POINTS** | **CATEGORIES** |
| **10** | **AI Integration Depth** |
|  | Meaningful use of Gemini / AI APIs as core logic (not just add-on). |
| **7** | **Agentic / Workflow Design** |
|  | Use of AI agents, RAG, automation flows, multi-step reasoning |
| **5** | **Technical Complexity** |
|  | Advanced architecture, scalability, system design |
| **3** | **System Reliability** |
|  | Stable, functional, minimal bugs |

| Innovation & Creativity (20 marks) |  |
| :---: | ----- |
| **MAX POINTS** | **CRITERIA** |
| **8** | **Originality of Idea** |
|  | Unique idea or fresh perspective |
| **7** | **Creative Use of AI** |
|  | Smart, unexpected use of AI technologies |
| **5** | **Wow Factor** |
|  | Memorable, impressive solution |

| Impact & Problem Relevance (20 marks) |  |
| :---: | ----- |
| **MAX POINTS** | **CRITERIA** |
| **7** | **Problem Clarity** |
|  | Clear, well-defined real-world problem |
| **7** | **National Relevance (Alignment with Malaysia context / tracks)** |
|  | Directly contributes to the Strategic Alignment & National Agenda. Addresses specific local challenges relevant to the Malaysian community and ecosystem. Focuses on moving from being a "technology consumer" to a "technology creator." |
| **6** | **Potential Real-World** |
|  | The solution is practical and demonstrates a clear path to real-world application. Potential for significant positive change (e.g., efficiency, accessibility, or sustainability). Evidence of social, economic, or environmental value to the target users. |

| UI/UX & Presentation (10 marks) |  |
| :---: | ----- |
| **MAX POINTS** | **CRITERIA** |
| **4** | **Interface Design** |
|  | Clean, professional, and modern UI that follows design best practices. Consistent use of typography, color schemes, and spacing. |
| **4** | **User Experience Flow** |
|  | The app is intuitive; users can achieve their goals with minimal friction. Logical progression from one screen/state to the next. |
| **2** | **Accessibility & Responsiveness** |
|  | High contrast, legible text, and usable by people with different abilities. The interface is fully responsive, looking and functioning perfectly on mobile, tablet, and desktop. |

| Code Quality (15 marks) |  |
| :---: | ----- |
| **MAX POINTS** | **CRITERIA** |
| **6** | **Code Organisation & Readability** |
|  | Code is well-structured, modular, and easy for another developer to read. Includes clear comments and documentation. |
| **5** | **Best Practices & Security** |
|  | Uses environment variables for secrets (no hardcoded keys). Includes input validation, error handling, and secure authentication practices. |
| **4** | **README & Documentation** |
|  | README includes clear setup steps, feature lists, and an architectural overview. |

| Pitch / Video Submission (10 marks) |  |
| :---: | ----- |
| **MAX POINTS** | **CRITERIA** |
| **4** | **Clarity of Explanation** |
|  | A crisp, compelling narrative that clearly defines the "Why" and "How." |
| **4** | **Demo Quality** |
|  | A live or recorded demo that successfully showcases all key features without failure. |
| **2** | **Team Presentation** |
|  | Confident, engaging, and well-rehearsed presentation by the team members. |

**Summary Marksheet**

| Category (Marks) |
| :---: |
| **AI Implementation & Technical Execution (25 Marks)** |
| **Innovation & Creativity (20 Marks)** |
| **Impact & Problem Relevance (20 Marks)** |
| **UI/UX & Product Experience (10 Marks)** |
| **Code Quality (15 Marks)** |
| **Pitch / Demo (10 Marks)** |

# **Prizes & Awards** {#prizes-&-awards}

* A Certificate and Digital of participation will be issued to all participants who were part of a registered team and successfully submitted their project.  
* Prizes will be distributed to all eligible participants once all competition rounds are finalized.  
* The final prize pool details will be announced soon—stay tuned\!


# **Conclusion** {#conclusion}

**WelcomeTo Project 2030: MyAI Future Hackathon by Google Develop On Campus UTM\!**

This is not like one of those technical competitions. A stage for students and developers out there to contribute to Malaysia's technological sovereignty and encourage yourself in securing your place as leading digital architect for this year  by “***Advancing The Nation, Building Solutions With Google AI”.***

You aren’t just tackling these National Grand Challenges, you are engineering solutions for the actual real life problems that are faced by millions of citizens. Creating a solution that is aligned with the National Agenda while mastering the Google AI Ecosystem, is credential that will set participants apart in the growing global AI economy. This is the moment where theory meets national impact and with the betterment of AI.

Don’t miss out on the chance. The tools are in your hand, the problems are real and the nation is watching forward. Let your imagination run wild and soak up every second of this development journey. Your breakthrough moment for Malaysia’s digital future is just around the corner, ***”Navigate AI for local impact and Build For the Good of Humanity”***. 

Wishing everyone who is taking part\! It's time for us to be the builder of the future with Project 2030: MyAI Future Hackathon.
